metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label is declared in `infra/gerrit/project.config` (with value, copyCondition, and ACL grant) before this story ships, or add its definition to this story's scope; the AC1 queue-selection query `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` cannot work without it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` as the queue-selection query (AC1), but no `Autosubmit` label exists in `infra/gerrit/project.config` or anywhere else in the codebase. The query targets a non-existent Gerrit label, so the selection mechanism described in AC1 would return zero results against the live Gerrit instance.
findings.location: AC1: 'Selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain (FIFO, oldest-submittable-first).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `[label "Autosubmit"]` block to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (value = 0 No score / +1 Land this change; copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE; function = NoBlock) and a corresponding `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Service Users` ACL grant in `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, or split label provisioning into an explicit prerequisite story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC2 the concrete decision rule for selecting the rebase endpoint: specify which Gerrit API field(s) to inspect (e.g. check `GET /changes/{id}/related` for a non-empty chain; check the change's commit for a merge commit to select `--no-ff` path) and define behavior for the overlapping case where a merge commit is a chain tip.
findings.finding: The plan specifies two rebase endpoint paths — `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` for 'ordinary + `--no-ff` merge changes' and `POST /rebase:chain` for 'relation chains' — but provides no decision rule for how the service determines which category a given change belongs to. A feature-branch merge commit that is ALSO the tip of a relation chain is both a `--no-ff` merge change and the head of a chain; the two categories are not mutually exclusive, and the Gerrit API field(s) to inspect (e.g. `has_related_changes`, change kind, subject) are unspecified.
findings.location: AC2: 'Rebase to tip: `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` for ordinary + `--no-ff` merge changes … `POST /rebase:chain` for relation chains'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label (value vocabulary, function, copyCondition, ACL) in `infra/gerrit/project.config` (or cross-reference the story that does so) so that AC1's query `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` resolves to a real label.
findings.finding: The `Autosubmit` label referenced in AC1 (`label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable`) does not exist anywhere in the codebase — it is absent from infra/gerrit/project.config and from every other config file — making it an undefined-but-referenced schema entity that an executor cannot implement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `[label "Autosubmit"]` stanza to infra/gerrit/project.config (e.g. `function = NoBlock`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Autosubmit requested`, bot-only ACL) and grant the auto-lander service account `label-Autosubmit = +1` on `refs/heads/*`, or explicitly cross-reference the predecessor story that provisions this label.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `wipChain` schema (fields: e.g., root change ID, chain member IDs, rebased-tip SHA, rebase-start timestamp) and its lifecycle transitions (set on rebase API call, cleared on successful submit or on explicit failure/abort) so the serialization invariant can be implemented and verified.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` state slot (AC5) has no defined schema, no specified transitions (when it is set, when cleared, and what it stores), and no placement in the service — leaving its structure and lifecycle ambiguous for an implementer.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5; What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC (e.g., a `pytest` invocation for a unit test, a `curl` to the Gerrit query API, or a structured log assertion) so each criterion has a deterministic pass/fail signal.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one live/end-to-end acceptance criterion (or explicitly name a child ticket) that exercises the actual Gerrit rebase+submit boundary — not solely an offline mock — so the happy-path loop is validated against a real or sandboxed Gerrit instance before the story closes.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new live Gerrit API boundaries (POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit, Verified vote polling) but specifies no testing strategy at all — not even a thin acceptance test. With no stated live/end-to-end criterion, the story can be closed green with offline-mock-only tests that never exercise the actual Gerrit submit path, satisfying all ACs while leaving the live integration path completely untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) / Scope section — testing approach absent throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A smoke/integration test against a test Gerrit instance (or replay fixture capturing real Gerrit HTTP responses) executes the full select→rebase→await-Verified→submit loop and asserts the submitted SHA matches the rebased patchset SHA.' If live testing is deferred, name the child ticket explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or a Verification section describing a smoke/integration test against a real or staging Gerrit instance (or explicitly document why this is deferred and name the child story that covers it).
findings.finding: No end-to-end check is described for the happy-path core loop as it would run against a real (or realistic staging) Gerrit instance — the plan introduces a new automated code path (rebase → await CI → submit) but provides no criterion that exercises this path live, only behavioral assertions.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution'; Acceptance Criteria as a whole

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the sort field for FIFO ordering in AC1 (e.g., 'sort by `created` timestamp ascending, falling back to change number').
findings.finding: The FIFO 'oldest-submittable-first' ordering key is unspecified — an executor cannot implement deterministic queue ordering without knowing which field defines 'oldest' (change creation timestamp, submit-ready timestamp, change number, etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'Selects the front label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable change/chain (FIFO, oldest-submittable-first).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'FIFO = sorted by Gerrit `created` timestamp ascending (lowest change number as tiebreak).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'fresh' in AC3 as a concrete predicate (e.g., 'Verified +1 vote whose patchset number equals the patchset created by this service's rebase call').
findings.finding: The definition of 'fresh Verified +1' is unspecified — the plan does not state whether 'fresh' means (a) the vote was cast on the current (rebased) patchset, (b) the vote was cast after the service issued the rebase, or (c) the vote timestamp is after patchset creation. Without this, the await condition cannot be implemented unambiguously.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1 on the rebased patchset before submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3: 'fresh = Verified +1 vote with `patch_set.number` equal to the patchset number returned by the rebase API call, not carried from a prior patchset.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full wipChain lifecycle: how it is created (on selection), how it is retired on happy-path submit success, and what the safe initial state is on service startup (e.g., always start with wipChain=None and re-select from queue).
findings.finding: The lifecycle of `wipChain` on termination paths (successful submit, rebase conflict, submit rejection, service crash mid-loop) is not defined; there is no stated retirement or recovery path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Single wipChain / single instance (serialized)' bullet; Scope — 'Failure paths … are separate stories'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'On service startup, wipChain is initialized to None (stateless restart); wipChain is cleared atomically after a confirmed submit response or when a failure path releases it (covered by failure-paths story, but startup assumption must be stated here).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the new auto-lander service may `import rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client` (reuse existing auth/transport/XSSI) or must implement its own Gerrit REST primitives; if the former, note that new endpoints (rebase, submit) extend the existing client rather than rebuild it.
findings.finding: The plan scopes the new service to 'project infra — NOT src/rebar core', but leaves ambiguous whether it may import from rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client. The new service needs Gerrit REST primitives (HTTP basic-auth, XSSI-strip, GerritError, urllib transport) that are already fully implemented in src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (6 existing production call-sites). If 'NOT src/rebar core' is read as 'cannot import rebar.*', the implementer will rebuild these primitives from scratch — a clear NIH violation against a production module.
findings.location: What / Scope sections: 'A project service (Python, project infra — NOT `src/rebar` core)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Scope: 'The service imports and extends `rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client.GerritClient` with rebase/submit endpoints; it is a standalone process in `infra/` but is not prohibited from importing `rebar.*` libraries.' This resolves the ambiguity without changing the architectural intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly whether `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` will be extended with rebase/submit methods (making it a named edit target) or whether the new service will contain a standalone Gerrit HTTP client.
findings.finding: The existing `GerritClient` in `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` has no `rebase`, `rebase_chain`, or `submit` methods. AC2 and AC4 require `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`, `POST /rebase:chain`, and `POST /submit` calls. The plan does not enumerate `gerrit_client.py` as an edit target, nor does it state whether the new service will extend this client or create a separate one.
findings.location: AC2: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase … POST /rebase:chain'; AC4: 'ancestor-atomic submit … POST /submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact POST path and request body for chain-rebase on Gerrit 3.14.1 (probe live or cite the release-specific REST API docs), and verify whether on_behalf_of_uploader is supported in the chain variant.
findings.finding: The plan names POST /rebase:chain as the endpoint for rebasing relation chains, but this endpoint has never been called anywhere in the codebase and is Unverified against Gerrit 3.14.1. The correct Gerrit REST path for chain-rebase is POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain (introduced in Gerrit 3.5+), but the plan's shorthand 'POST /rebase:chain' omits the change-scoped prefix, leaving the exact surface unconfirmed.
findings.location: AC2: 'POST /rebase:chain for relation chains'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete freshness-detection rule to AC3 — e.g., 'Verified +1 is considered fresh when the vote's `patch_set` number equals the current revision's patch-set number as returned by `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS`' — so the polling protocol is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC3 specifies 'awaits a fresh `Verified +1` on the rebased patchset' but provides no decision protocol for what constitutes 'fresh' — specifically, no rule for how the service determines that the Verified vote it observes was cast on the post-rebase revision rather than an earlier patchset or a carried-over vote.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to read: '…awaits `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` returning `labels.Verified.approved.patch_set == current_revision.patch_set_number` with value +1 before proceeding to submit.' This ties freshness to the Gerrit API field that already encodes the patchset, eliminating timestamp-based heuristics.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate the Gerrit first-party `autosubmit` plugin as prior art: confirm whether it can be deployed on Gerrit 3.14.1 and whether it satisfies the 'await fresh Verified +1 on rebased patchset before submit' requirement; if it does not (e.g. it submits immediately on label without waiting for CI on the rebased PS), document why the bespoke service is needed instead.
findings.finding: The plan names its opt-in label 'Autosubmit' — the identical name used by Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` plugin (used in Chromium's Gerrit) — but never acknowledges, evaluates, or dismisses the plugin as prior art. If the plugin can be deployed on the existing Gerrit 3.14.1 instance, it may satisfy the use case without a bespoke Python service.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Selects the front label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable change/chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Alternatives considered' note in the plan's Why/What section: 'The Gerrit autosubmit plugin submits as soon as is:submittable without re-running CI on the rebased patchset, which violates R5 (FFO + TRIVIAL_REBASE drops Verified, so the plugin would submit before the re-CI pass completes). This service closes that gap by awaiting a fresh Verified +1 on the rebased SHA.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact Gerrit REST path for batch chain-rebase (it may be `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` per Gerrit docs, or require the `rebase` plugin) and record the verified endpoint URL and minimum Gerrit version in the AC or a linked reference.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /rebase:chain` as the endpoint for rebasing relation chains (AC2), but this endpoint string does not appear anywhere in the codebase (docs, infra, src). The only Gerrit rebase endpoint evidenced in the codebase (`gerrit_client.py`) is `POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/review`; the batch chain-rebase REST call is unverified.
findings.location: AC2: '`POST /rebase:chain` for relation chains'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the single-instance enforcement mechanism in AC5 (e.g., 'in-process asyncio lock within a single long-running process' or 'OS-level PID file lock').
findings.finding: The single-instance / serialization enforcement mechanism is left as an unresolved design choice for the executor — the plan states the requirement ('single wipChain / single instance') but names no concrete mechanism (process lock, advisory DB lock, in-process mutex, distributed lock, etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5: 'Single wipChain / single instance (serialized) — never rebases multiple changes onto the same HEAD concurrently.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a constraint bullet specifying the Gerrit auth mechanism (e.g., 'HTTP basic auth via `GERRIT_HTTP_PASSWORD` env var for service account `autosubmit-bot`') and confirm the account holds the Forge Author capability needed for `on_behalf_of_uploader=true`.
findings.finding: Gerrit API authentication / credential provisioning is entirely unmentioned — the plan calls multiple authenticated Gerrit endpoints (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase`, `POST /submit`, `on_behalf_of_uploader`) but does not state how credentials are obtained, stored, or rotated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2 and AC4 (Gerrit API calls); 'What' section ('project service, Python, project infra')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC4 that the `is:submittable` re-verify is an advisory early-exit (avoids a wasted POST /submit), not the atomicity lock — the actual guard is Gerrit's FFO submit-type rejection, and the service must handle the submit API's error response (e.g. 409 Conflict) when tip has moved between check and call.
findings.finding: The plan's AC4 describes 're-verify `is:submittable` immediately before `POST /submit`' as though this re-verify is the atomicity guard for 'exact tested SHA,' but the actual guard is Gerrit's FFO submit type rejecting any submit whose change is not at the current tip. The plan does not explain that the re-verify is advisory (a wasted-call optimization), not a safety lock — an implementer may believe the check provides the safety and be confused when concurrent submissions still fail at Gerrit, or may over-engineer a retry loop expecting the check to be authoritative.
findings.location: AC4: 'ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA; re-verify `is:submittable` immediately before `POST /submit`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'The `is:submittable` re-verify is an advisory fast-fail; the submit endpoint itself enforces FFO atomicity and will return an error if tip moved — the service must handle that response and re-enter the loop (covered by the failure-paths story).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike/probe task to empirically verify that `POST /rebase:chain` with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` correctly preserves `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers on a test chain before committing to the implementation.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` on `rebase:chain` will correctly preserve DCO `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers on rebased patchsets, but includes no spike or fixture to validate this specific Gerrit feature combination before full build-out.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff policy (e.g., exponential backoff with cap) for all Gerrit API calls, and define a maximum wait timeout for the Verified-polling loop with an explicit failure action (e.g., abandon wipChain, alert, retry next cycle).
findings.finding: No retry, backoff, or timeout is specified for any external Gerrit API call or for the 'await Verified +1' polling loop; if Gerrit is slow or the CI system stalls, the service hangs indefinitely with no circuit-breaker or degraded-mode behavior.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1' and 'Ancestor-atomic submit' bullets
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'All Gerrit API calls use exponential backoff (3 retries, max 30s delay); the Verified-await loop has a configurable timeout (default 2h) after which wipChain is released and the change is re-queued or flagged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability requirements: specify that each stage transition (selected, rebased, awaiting-CI, submitted, failed) emits a structured log event and at least one metric (e.g., counter per stage outcome), and define an alert condition for wipChain stuck longer than N minutes.
findings.finding: No observability requirements (metrics, structured logs, or alerts) are specified for the core loop's failure points, making it impossible for operators to detect hangs, rebase failures, or submit errors in production.
findings.location: What / Scope sections — no monitoring or instrumentation mentioned

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the TOCTOU window between re-verify and submit, and specify the response to a submit rejection (e.g., release wipChain, log the event, re-queue the change for next iteration).
findings.finding: The re-verify `is:submittable` check immediately before `POST /submit` is a check-then-act (TOCTOU) sequence with no atomicity guarantee; Gerrit does not offer a conditional submit API, but the plan does not acknowledge this race or specify a safe response when submit is rejected due to a concurrent state change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA; re-verify is:submittable immediately before POST /submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify Gerrit supports `RebaseInput.on_behalf_of_uploader` on the deployed version, add `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = group Service Users` to `infra/gerrit/project.config [access "refs/heads/*"]`, and add an AC or note confirming that this ACL grant is in scope for this story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` in the rebase API call will 'preserve author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer' (AC2), but: (a) `on_behalf_of_uploader` is not referenced anywhere in the codebase (docs, infra, src), (b) the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` ACL it requires is absent from `infra/gerrit/project.config`, and (c) no evidence confirms the auto-lander bot account holds this permission.
findings.location: AC2: '`on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = group Service Users` to the `[access "refs/heads/*"]` block in `infra/gerrit/project.config` and reference the Gerrit version that introduced `RebaseInput.on_behalf_of_uploader` (3.6+) to confirm compatibility.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify experimentally (against the deployed Gerrit 3.14.1 instance) that `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase:chain` accepts and honours `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` in the `RebaseInput` body, and document the result (e.g. in a new fixtures file or a note in the relevant ADR); if the field is not supported on chain-rebase, specify the fallback (per-change rebase with the field, or a trailer-fixup step).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `POST /rebase:chain` (chain rebase) accepts `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` in the `RebaseInput` body — but this is an unverified capability claim. The Gerrit REST docs document `on_behalf_of_uploader` for single-change `/rebase`, and its availability/behavior for the chain-rebase endpoint on the deployed Gerrit 3.14.1 has not been confirmed anywhere in the codebase or ADRs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'POST /rebase:chain for relation chains; on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `RebaseInput.on_behalf_of_uploader` is operative on Gerrit 3.14.1 (probe the live instance or release notes), confirm the bot's service account requires a `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` ACL grant not currently in `project.config`, and add that ACL stanza to `infra/gerrit/project.config` before shipping.
findings.finding: The plan asserts POST /changes/{id}/rebase with on_behalf_of_uploader=true to preserve author/DCO/Signed-off-by trailers, but (a) this RebaseInput field has never been called in the codebase (absent from gerrit_client.py), (b) it requires a Gerrit 'rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader' permission that is not present in infra/gerrit/project.config, and (c) its interaction with requireSignedOffBy=true (project.config line 40) on Gerrit 3.14.1 is unverified against the live instance. This is a new integration surface whose critical behavioral dependency — DCO/trailer preservation — is Unverified.
findings.location: AC2: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a smoke-probe in the e2e harness that calls POST /a/changes/{test-change-id}/rebase with {"on_behalf_of_uploader": true} under the bot token and confirms HTTP 200 + preserved author. Then add the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` permission to the [access "refs/heads/*"] block in project.config for the auto-lander bot's group.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the auth precondition for POST /changes/{id}/submit: name the Gerrit group the auto-lander bot account will be added to (Contributors, a new 'Autolanders' group, or Administrators), add that ACL stanza to project.config, and confirm it does not conflict with the exclusive Submit permission.
findings.finding: The plan adds POST /changes/{id}/submit as the bot-initiated submit endpoint ('ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA') but this endpoint is not currently used in the codebase. No auth precondition is stated for it: specifically, the bot's service account must hold the 'Submit' ACL on refs/heads/*, which is currently restricted to the 'Contributors' group + Administrators (project.config lines 63-64). If the auto-lander bot's service account is not in 'Contributors', every POST /submit will return 403.
findings.location: AC4: 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA; re-verify is:submittable immediately before POST /submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Create a dedicated Gerrit group (e.g., 'Autolanders') and add 'submit = group Autolanders' to [access "refs/heads/*"] in project.config alongside the existing Contributors grant; add the bot account to that group via setup-project.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence in the What or Scope section naming LLM-Review as an external dependency (per ADR-0041) that auto-votes Verified on trivial-rebase patchsets, so implementers understand AC3's await loop has a prerequisite outside this service.
findings.finding: The entity 'LLM-Review' is referenced in AC3 as the actor that 'carries TRIVIAL_REBASE/MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE per ADR-0041' and causes 'Verified re-runs', but this entity is not described anywhere in the Why, What, or Scope sections. It is a load-bearing dependency (the entire await-Verified step depends on LLM-Review re-voting automatically), yet no plan text explains what it is, who operates it, or that it is a precondition for this service to work correctly.
findings.location: AC3: 'LLM-Review carries TRIVIAL_REBASE/MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE per ADR-0041; Verified re-runs'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'Depends on LLM-Review (ADR-0041) auto-casting Verified on TRIVIAL_REBASE/MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE patchsets; the await loop in AC3 polls until that vote arrives.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the pre-submit is:submittable re-verification step to the What section so it is described as a deliverable and not only implied by AC4.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies a pre-submit guard — 're-verify is:submittable immediately before POST /submit' — that is not described as work to be done in the What or Scope sections. The What only states 'ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA' with no mention of a final submittability re-check, leaving this step as an orphan AC requirement.
findings.location: AC4: 're-verify is:submittable immediately before POST /submit'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend the submit description to: 'Before submitting, re-queries is:submittable to confirm the change is still submittable, then ancestor-atomically submits the exact tested SHA via POST /submit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task to create an ADR (or CLAUDE.md section) capturing the wipChain serialization model, FIFO ordering rationale, Phase-1 no-vote constraint, and the rebase-chain→await-Verified→ancestor-atomic-submit contract.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel project service with a new architectural contract (serialized wipChain auto-lander loop) and references ADR-0041, but does not call for a new ADR or design-doc entry for the new service/pattern itself.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a story task: 'Write ADR-XXXX: Serial Auto-Lander Architecture' covering (1) wipChain singleton and why concurrency is excluded, (2) FIFO ordering policy, (3) Phase-1 read-only Verified stance, (4) on_behalf_of_uploader attribution contract.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that label names (`Autosubmit`, `Verified`, `LLM-Review`), the FIFO ordering policy, and Phase-1 behavioral flags are read from a config object/file rather than hardcoded in the loop logic.
findings.finding: The FIFO ordering policy ('oldest-submittable-first') and Gerrit label names (`Autosubmit+1`, `Verified +1`, `LLM-Review`) are referenced as hardcoded rules with no mention of making them configurable, yet the plan's own scope notes these are Phase 1 decisions implying future evolution.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC3, AC6; ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `AutoladerConfig` dataclass (or TOML section) with fields: `submit_label`, `verified_label`, `ordering` (enum: FIFO|PRIORITY), `cast_votes` (bool). Reference it from all AC implementations.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs or Definition of Done — e.g., a before/after workflow comparison (log showing zero concurrent wipChain conflicts in a test run), an operational metric target, or a dogfooding checkpoint — so the 'stop racing' value claim is verifiably met.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is specified for the value claim: the plan states that agents currently 'hand-roll and race' but provides no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric (e.g., zero concurrent rebase attempts), or dogfooding checkpoint to confirm the problem is actually solved.
findings.location: Why section / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Integration test or log evidence shows that under concurrent Autosubmit requests, only one wipChain is active at a time (zero concurrent rebase-to-tip attempts on the same HEAD).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete file path / module / package where the new auto-lander service lives (e.g., `infra/autolander/lander.py` or `src/rebar/auto_lander/`) so the edit target can be grepped and verified.
findings.finding: The plan names no concrete file paths, module name, or package location for the new service ('project infra — NOT src/rebar core'). No matching artifact exists in the repo (searches for auto_lander, autolander, lander.py, wipChain, rebase:chain all return zero code hits). An implementer cannot locate or verify the edit target.
findings.location: What section: 'A project service (Python, project infra — NOT src/rebar core)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the 'What' or a new 'Approach' section explaining why the synchronous serial loop with a single `wipChain` was chosen over event-driven alternatives (e.g. Gerrit SSH event stream), citing simplicity, operational blast-radius, and alignment with the existing project-infra pattern.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' and 'Why' sections state the goal (automate FFO rebase-to-submit, serialize to prevent racing) but provide no positive rationale for the chosen architectural shape: a Python project-infra service with a synchronous serial loop holding a single `wipChain`. The plan does not state why a synchronous loop is preferred over, e.g., an event-driven Gerrit stream-listener or a simple scheduled job, leaving future maintainers without design intent.
